_meta.title: Daily RevOps Report — canonical source-per-metric registry
_meta.purpose: The SINGLE machine-readable map of metric -> {source type, exact id, tab, desk/owner, dedup key, reconciliation anchor} for the Daily RevOps Report. scripts/render_daily.py resolves its human-managed / drift-prone sources (Pre-IPO desks, booking sheet, sendivo sub-accounts, workspace roster) FROM this file, so registry = code = reality (no comment-vs-code drift). A chat building or QAing the report reads THIS to answer 'am I sourcing the right thing for this metric?' WITHOUT a human hand-feeding it. Run `render_daily.py <date> --verify` to confirm every source is live, correctly-shaped, and (for Pre-IPO) reconciles to the team tally.

schema.source_type: sheet | warehouse | api  — where the metric is read from
schema.sheet: { spreadsheet_id, tab, date_column, dedup_key, owner } (Google Sheet)
schema.warehouse: { relation, key_columns } (DuckDB serving snapshot via read API)
schema.dedup_key: the field(s) collapsed to one booking, e.g. 'email|phone' = lower(email) else phone
schema.reconciliation: { type, source, pattern, rule } — the independent tally a render reconciles to so drift is flagged, not silently rendered (NULL = structural health-gate only)

_meta.change_capture: When a desk/sheet is ADDED, RENAMED, or a sub-account/key changes: update THIS file in the same PR that touches the renderer (or before). Never let the report discover drift by Sam eyeballing a wrong number. Pre-IPO desks specifically: the authoritative desk roster is owned by the #pre-ipo-success team (see open_questions[0]); add a desk here the moment it appears in that channel.
_meta.self_verify_protocol: A QA/build chat confirms a metric with zero hand-feeding by: (1) reading this registry for the metric's declared source(s); (2) running `render_daily.py <date> --verify` which probes each source is reachable + has its declared columns + parses non-degenerate, and reconciles Pre-IPO per-desk counts to the anchor; (3) any DRIFT / UNREACHABLE / column-missing prints LOUDLY and the verify exits non-zero (drift is flagged, never silently rendered).

workspaces._note: Workspace identity is shared across §1 Instantly key slug, §4 account_label.workspace_slug, §6 email_message.workspace_id, and core.workspace.slug — ONE slug end-to-end. Render order = list order. renaissance-2 is Funding 5 (Eyver) for EMAIL; it is ALSO the SMS Pre-IPO sub-account (13922) — do not conflate the two channels.

email_sent_opps.what: Email sent + opportunities per workspace. This ONE fetch also feeds §4 'Actual' (so §1 sent and §4 Actual can never disagree).
wrong_sources: pipeline raw_pipeline_campaign_daily_metrics drops 140k-270k sends/day into a NULL bucket (Funding 5 read 91k vs real 232k)

reconciliation.rule: a REPORTING workspace absent from the API result renders 0 sent/opps (wrong-but-plausible zero, e.g. dead/rotated key) -> WARN loudly, never silent.

email_meetings_leadtype.what: Email-channel meetings per workspace (count) + cheap-vs-regular MCA split.

sheet.owner: portal-fed consolidated booking sheet (Channel + Workspace + Partner per booking)

leadtype_source.rule: offer='Funding', cheap = lead_type='cheap', joined to booking by email|phone

wrong_sources: sendivo delivery_metrics returns the DEFAULT account (~178) regardless of sub_account_id — NOT the SMS-sent source

sms_replies_human.what: Human (non-opt-out) inbound SMS replies per sub-account.

whatsapp.what: WhatsApp sent / delivered / failed / replies (ISKRA 'sent' runs ~30-37% failed; KPI = delivered/mtg).

preipo_meetings.section: §2 Ren2 (SMS · Pre-IPO) meetings + yellow block 'SMS IPO'
preipo_meetings.what: Pre-IPO ('SMS IPO') meetings = the sum of bookings across the active Pre-IPO booking DESKS. THIS is the metric that the data ticket was opened for: the desks were not enumerated/labeled in a way a chat could confirm, and a human had to hand-feed 'Collins + Summit'.
preipo_meetings.additive_rule: A lead books ONE desk or the other (Collins overflow -> Summit, per Grace). Desks are ADDITIVE; count each desk independently and SUM. Do NOT cross-dedup between desks. Dedup ONLY within each desk by email|phone.
preipo_meetings.count_rule: Count rows whose 'Date' (booking-made date) == report day, deduped within the desk by email|phone.

desks.owner: Collins desk (advisor names per the sheet: Tsvi Bort / Lawrence Michelson — NOT stated in Slack, treat as sheet-internal)
desks.label_provenance: Grace -> Sam DM 2026-06-29 explicitly maps 1IZzmCXt… = collins; Ido posted this sheet in #pre-ipo-success 2026-04-27.

Grace -> Sam DM 2026-06-29 explicitly maps 1oKlY_2qI… = summit; Ido posted it in #pre-ipo-chat 2026-06-10. The earlier 'Craig Diana' label was WRONG ('Craig' was only an advisor first-name at setup); do NOT re-mislabel this sheet.

wrong_sources: the consolidated 1vaExhxu sheet does NOT carry the Pre-IPO desks

known_good.source: #pre-ipo-success per-desk counter (Jamil Matias run, peaked Collins 23 / Summit 11); matches both sheets.

reconciliation.line_regex_note: Desk-AGNOSTIC on purpose: it captures '<Word> Booked <N>' at the start of a line, so a NEW desk posting in the channel (the way 'Summit' was once new + uncaptured) is detected as an unknown-desk drift signal. Known vs unknown is decided against desks[] in code, not baked into the regex. Anchored to line-start AND the count must be followed by a non-letter (separator/end-of-line) via negative lookahead — so a freeform sentence line like 'Summit Booked 99 leads in pipeline' or 'We Booked 3 meetings' does NOT corrupt a desk's counter.
reconciliation.rule: Each desk posts a running counter that RESETS to 1 each ET calendar day, and EACH BOOKER runs their own independent 1..N. So take MAX N per (desk, booker) within the report ET day (by message POST date — NOT the 'June DD' script-name in the text), then SUM across bookers per desk (two bookers each reaching 11 = 22, not max=11; one booker's run split over messages still collapses to their single max). Reconcile that to the rendered per-desk sheet count. On mismatch FLAG drift. A desk label seen in the channel that is NOT in desks[] is itself drift (a new/renamed desk -> update this registry).
reconciliation.caveats: There is NO canonical desk list / pinned roster in Slack and NO end-of-day grand-total message — the per-booker counter is the only signal (see open_questions[0]). LIMITATION: this reads only top-level channel messages (Slack conversations.history), so if a desk ever posts its counter as a THREAD reply it would be missed and reconciliation would false-flag drift (the live 2026-06-29 run confirmed desks post top-level). Darcy is wiring an automated 'Renny' bot (~2026-06-30) to post all future bookings tagged with partner + channel; when it lands the line format may change and this regex/rule may need updating. In all cases the rendered Pre-IPO number comes from the desk SHEETS (source of truth) — this anchor only decides whether a drift alert fires.
reconciliation.degrade: If Slack creds/scopes are unavailable, --verify falls back to known_good when the verify date matches, else reports ANCHOR_UNAVAILABLE (explicit, not a pass). The structural health-gate (reachable + Date column present + non-degenerate parse) ALWAYS runs.

close_calls.what: Dials / distinct leads / connects (>=60s real convo, ET day). Close is the system of record; it UNDER-captures vs the warm-callers' own EOD tallies — note it, do not 'fix' it.

sending_truth_expected.what: Expected = sum of ACTIVE accounts' configured daily_limit per workspace x infra (OTD/Google; Outlook excluded), latest census, same-day (no lag).

warehouse.filter: lifecycle='Active' AND infra IN ('OTD','Google'), census_date = max

sending_truth_expected.actual_source: Instantly daily per workspace = the SAME fetch as metrics.email_sent_opps (so §4 Actual == §1 sent)
wrong_sources: raw_account_truth_daily_actuals lags 2-3d (the '150k on a June-29 tab' bug)

im_reply_time.what: First-reply latency per workspace (daily/weekly/monthly): first prospect reply (ue_type 2) -> first our reply (ue_type 3) per thread.
wrong_sources: raw_pipeline_conversation_messages chain froze iam_response_time / v_sla_reply_time_daily at 2026-06-23
im_reply_time.pending: SMS + WhatsApp first-reply time not yet computed (rendered 'pending')

open_questions.question: Is there a single canonical/authoritative list of ALL active Pre-IPO booking desks + their Google Sheet IDs (so a new desk like 'Summit' is captured automatically next time), or does each desk just have its own sheet known only ad-hoc in Slack? And will the team commit to updating that list (and this registry) when a desk is added/renamed? Specifically: will the new 'Renny' automation (Darcy, ~2026-06-30) become the authoritative, machine-readable booking source for Pre-IPO (with partner + channel + desk per booking) — and if so, should the renderer read Pre-IPO meetings FROM Renny's output instead of the two desk sheets?
open_questions.why: Confirmed 2026-06-30 via Slack: there is NO pinned roster/canvas/doc in #pre-ipo-success and NO auto-discoverable desk list — a newly-added desk only appears as a new '<Desk> Booked' label in the post stream, so it would silently drop out of the Pre-IPO total until Sam eyeballs a wrong number. This registry's preipo_meetings.desks must equal that roster. The Renny automation may make this whole sheet-scrape obsolete.
open_questions.status: OPEN — routed to Sam 2026-06-30 (to relay to Grace/Darcy); registered in reference_people_pending_asks_20260629
